references.text: [Er95] Erd\H{o}s, Paul, Some of my favourite problems in number theory, combinatorics, and geometry. Resenhas (1995), 165-186.

remarks.text: Erd\H{o}s \cite{Er95} could prove the distribution function is purely singular.

statement.text: Schoenberg proved that for every $c\in [0,1]$ the density of\[\{ n\in \mathbb{N} : \phi(n)<cn\}\]exists. Let this density be denoted by $f(c)$. Is it true that there are no $x$ such that $f'(x)$ exists and is positive?
